children.name: Amphisbaena alba
children.description: Amphisbaena alba, also known as the red worm lizard A. alba has a diverse diet ranging from plant material to small vertebrates Numerically, beetles, ants, and spiders compose the majority of their diet; however, ants, insect larvae, beetles, cockroaches, hemipterans, mole crickets, crickets, grasshoppers, termites, spiders, scorpions, pseudoscorpions, and annelids are ingested to satisfy a larger volume.
